summary:
• Iran's tentative 60-day truce renewal with the US reduces near-term sanctions risk, but geopolitical uncertainty remains elevated given the fragility of negotiations and potential for rapid escalation affecting crude supply.

• Norway's campaign against EU Arctic drilling restrictions and increased investment forecasts signal supply growth intentions in the North Atlantic, though regulatory headwinds could delay production increases and tighten Western Hemisphere crude balances.

• U.S. crude export surges to all-time highs combined with freefall inventory levels indicate tight domestic supply conditions that could constrain exports if refinery utilization declines or demand weakens, creating vulnerability to price shocks.
